title: Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu has work to do. He thinks: 'I have work to do. If I do it, my body will get tired. Let…
content: Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu has work to do. He thinks: 'I have work to do. If I do it, my body will get tired. Let me lie down.' He lies down, not making an effort to attain the unattained, to achieve the unachieved, to realize the unrealized. Bhikkhus, this is the first ground for laziness.

title: Elder Aṅgaṇika Bhāradvāja: In the forest I tended the sacred fire, following an incorrect tradition. Not knowing the path of purity, following the austere practice…
content:
Elder Aṅgaṇika Bhāradvāja:

In the forest I tended the sacred fire, following an incorrect tradition.
Not knowing the path of purity, following the austere practice of the Deathless.

With happiness, I attained happiness. See the Dhamma in accordance with the Dhamma.
I have attained the three knowledges; done is the Buddha's teaching.

Formerly a brahmin's relative, now I am truly a brahmin.
I am consecrated with the three knowledges; I have attained security.
I rightly understand the true Dhamma; I have mastered the Buddha's word.

title: When the bhikkhu is going forward or returning, he clearly comprehends what he is doing; when looking ahead or looking aside, he clearly comprehends what…
content: When the bhikkhu is going forward or returning, he clearly comprehends what he is doing; when looking ahead or looking aside, he clearly comprehends what he is doing; when bending or stretching his limbs, he clearly comprehends what he is doing ... Thus he dwells mindful and clearly comprehending, practicing mindfulness and clear comprehension.

title: 'Bhikkhus, develop mindfulness of breathing.' When this was said, the Venerable Ariṭṭha said to the Blessed One: 'Venerable Sir, I develop mindfulness of breathing.' -…
content: 'Bhikkhus, develop mindfulness of breathing.' When this was said, the Venerable Ariṭṭha said to the Blessed One: 'Venerable Sir, I develop mindfulness of breathing.' - 'Ariṭṭha, how do you develop mindfulness of breathing?' - 'Venerable Sir, regarding past sensual pleasures, I have abandoned desire and lust. Regarding future sensual pleasures, I have abandoned desire and lust. Regarding internal and external objects, the perception of aversion is well subdued in me. Mindfully I breathe in, mindfully I breathe out. Thus, Venerable Sir, I develop mindfulness of breathing.' - 'This also, Ariṭṭha, is mindfulness of breathing, but I declare that it is not. Ariṭṭha, I will teach you how mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated to perfection. Listen and pay close attention, I will speak.' - 'Yes, Venerable Sir.' Then the Blessed One said: 'Ariṭṭha, how is mindfulness of breathing developed and cultivated to perfection? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu goes to the forest ... "Contemplating relinquishment, I will breathe in," he trains; "Contemplating relinquishment, I will breathe out," he trains. Thus, Ariṭṭha, mindfulness of breathing is developed and cultivated to perfection.'

title: Here, bhikkhus, the bhikkhus learn the scriptures that are wrongly held, with misplaced words and phrases. Bhikkhus, when words and phrases are misplaced, the meaning…
content: Here, bhikkhus, the bhikkhus learn the scriptures that are wrongly held, with misplaced words and phrases. Bhikkhus, when words and phrases are misplaced, the meaning is wrongly interpreted. This is the first thing, bhikkhus, that leads to the confusion and disappearance of the good Dhamma. Furthermore, bhikkhus, the bhikkhus are difficult to speak to, possessing qualities that make them difficult to admonish, difficult to endure, not respectfully accepting instruction. This is the second thing, bhikkhus, that leads to the confusion and disappearance of the good Dhamma. Furthermore, bhikkhus, the learned bhikkhus who are the bearers of the tradition, the bearers of the Dhamma, the bearers of the Discipline, the bearers of the summaries, are not careful in having others teach the Dhamma. Because of this, the discourses are cut off at the root, no longer serving as a refuge. This is the third thing, bhikkhus, that leads to the confusion and disappearance of the good Dhamma.

title: This world and the next, The Wise One has well explained, Knowing Māra's realm, Beyond the reach of Death." The Perfectly Enlightened One, All-Knowing, Knows…
content:
This world and the next,
The Wise One has well explained,
Knowing Māra's realm,
Beyond the reach of Death."

The Perfectly Enlightened One, All-Knowing,
Knows all worlds,
The door to the Deathless is open,
Reached the security of Nibbāna.

The stream of Māra is cut,
Shattered and destroyed,
Therefore, bhikkhus,
Live with abundant joy,
Attain the security of Nibbāna.

title: Just as, bhikkhus, a man throws a yoke with a single hole into the great ocean. An easterly wind drives it westward, a westerly wind…
content:
Just as, bhikkhus, a man throws a yoke with a single hole into the great ocean. An easterly wind drives it westward, a westerly wind drives it eastward, a northerly wind drives it southward, a southerly wind drives it northward. There is a blind turtle that surfaces once every hundred years. Bhikkhus, what do you think? Would that blind turtle ever put its neck into that yoke with a single hole?" "If it ever does, Venerable Sir, it would be after a very long time." "More quickly, bhikkhus, could that blind turtle put its neck into that yoke with a single hole than a fool who has fallen into a plane of misery could regain human birth. Why is that? Because there, bhikkhus, there is no practice of Dhamma, no practice of peace, no wholesome practice, no meritorious practice. There, bhikkhus, they devour one another and prey on the weak.

And if, after a very long time, that fool regains human birth, he will be reborn in a low family, a family of outcasts, of hunters, of bamboo-weavers, of cart-makers, or of scavengers — a poor family with little food and drink, where even a rag for covering is hard to get. Moreover, he will be ugly, misshapen, dwarfish, sickly, blind, crippled, lame, or paralyzed, unable to get food, drink, clothing, vehicles, garlands, perfumes, bedding, lodgings, and light. He engages in misconduct of body, speech, and mind; because of that, after death, with the breakup of the body, he is reborn in a plane of misery, a bad destination, a lower realm, hell.

title: Bhikkhus, what is meant by 'some people are attached'? Here, bhikkhus, some devas and humans delight in existence. When the Dhamma for the cessation of…
content:
Bhikkhus, what is meant by 'some people are attached'? Here, bhikkhus, some devas and humans delight in existence. When the Dhamma for the cessation of existence is taught to them, their minds do not enter into it, do not become confident, do not become established, are not drawn to it. Thus, bhikkhus, some are attached.

And what, bhikkhus, are 'some people who overreach'? Here, bhikkhus, some people are troubled, ashamed, and disgusted with existence, and they delight in non-existence, saying: 'With the breakup of the body, this self is annihilated, destroyed, and does not exist after death.' This is their view, which is immediate, supreme, and true. Thus, bhikkhus, some overreach.

And what, bhikkhus, are 'some people with vision'? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu sees what has come to be as having come to be. Having seen what has come to be as having come to be, he practices for disenchantment, for dispassion, for cessation regarding it. Thus, bhikkhus, some have vision.

Those who have seen
What has come to be as it is,
And have seen the escape
From what has come to be,
Are liberated in accordance with what has come to be,
By the destruction of craving for becoming.

If a bhikkhu fully understands
What has come to be as it is,
Free from craving for becoming and non-becoming,
He will not come again to birth.

title: Behold this adorned appearance, With jewels and with earrings, A skeleton covered with skin, Made radiant by fine clothes.
content:
Behold this adorned appearance,
With jewels and with earrings,
A skeleton covered with skin,
Made radiant by fine clothes.

title: Elder Puṇṇa Mantānīputta: Associate with the wise, who see the goal, Great, deep, hard to see, subtle, fine, Attained by the wise, not heedless, attentive.
content:
Elder Puṇṇa Mantānīputta:

Associate with the wise, who see the goal,
Great, deep, hard to see, subtle, fine,
Attained by the wise, not heedless, attentive.
